devanagari: अजगरमुखात् अनन्दस्य मोचनम्अजगरस्य पूर्वविद्याधरप्राप्ति शंखचूड वधः । श्रीशुक उवाच । एकदा देवयात्रायां गोपाला जातकौतुकाः । अनोभिरनडुद्यु...
english_devnagari: ajagaramukhāt anandasya mocanamajagarasya pūrvavidyādharaprāpti śaṃkhacūḍa vadhaḥ śrīśuka uvāca ekadā devayātrāyāṃ gopālā jātakautukāḥ anobhiranaḍudyuktaiḥ prayayuste

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation:
Śrī Śuka said:
On one occasion, the cowherds had their curiosity aroused in participating in a pilgrimage to god (Śiva) and they set out in carts drawn by bullocks to a place known as Ambikāvana (the grove of goddess Pārvatī).

devanagari: तत्र स्नात्वा सरस्वत्यां देवं पशुपतिं विभुम् । आनर्चुरर्हणैर्भक्त्या देवीं च णृपतेऽम्बिकाम् ॥ २ ॥
english_devnagari: tatra snātvā sarasvatyāṃ devaṃ paśupatiṃ vibhum ānarcurarhaṇairbhaktyā devīṃ ca ṇṛpate
translation: Having performed ablutions in the river Sarasvatī, they devoutly worshipped the god Śiva as well as the goddess Pārvatī with various articles of worship.

devanagari: गावो हिरण्यं वासांसि मधु मध्वन्नमादृताः । ब्राह्मणेभ्यो ददुः सर्वे देवो नः प्रीयतामिति ॥ ३ ॥
english_devnagari: gāvo hiraṇyaṃ vāsāṃsi madhu madhvannamādṛtāḥ brāhmaṇebhyo daduḥ sarve devo naḥ prīyatāmiti

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [imperative active third dual], [imperative middle third single], [imperative passive third single]

translation: Most reverentially, they donated cows, gold, garments, honey, sweet-meats to Brāhmaṇas as gifts, praying “May god Śiva be propitious unto us.”

devanagari: ऊषुः सरस्वतीतीरे जलं प्राश्य यतव्रताः । रजनीं तां महाभागा नन्दसुनन्दकादयः ॥ ४ ॥
english_devnagari: ūṣuḥ sarasvatītīre jalaṃ prāśya yatavratāḥ rajanīṃ tāṃ mahābhāgā nandasunandakādayaḥ
translation: The highly fortunate Nanda, Sunandaka and others observed a vow of subsisting on the waters of the river (the whole day) and passed that night on the bank of the Sarasvatī.

devanagari: कश्चिन्महानहिस्तस्मिन्विपिनेऽतिबुभुक्षितः । यदृच्छयागतो नन्दं शयानमुरगोऽग्रसीत् ॥ ५ ॥
english_devnagari: kaścinmahānahistasminvipine yadṛcchayāgato nandaṃ śayānamurago
translation: A certain big boa-constricter of that forest, being pinched with extreme hunger, came there by chance and began to swallow up Nanda who was asleep.

devanagari: स चुक्रोशाहिना ग्रस्तः कृष्ण कृष्ण महानयम् । सर्पो मां ग्रसते तात प्रपन्नं परिमोचय ॥ ६ ॥
english_devnagari: sa cukrośāhinā grastaḥ kṛṣṇa kṛṣṇa mahānayam sarpo māṃ grasate tāta prapannaṃ parimocaya

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation: While being swallowed by the serpent, Nanda screamed out “Oh Kṛṣṇa, Kṛṣṇa, this big serpent is devouring me, Oh child! please save me who have approached you for rescue.”

devanagari: तस्य चाक्रन्दितं श्रुत्वा गोपालाः सहसोत्थिताः । ग्रस्तं च दृष्ट्वा विभ्रान्ताः सर्पं विव्यधुरुल्मुकैः ॥ ७ ॥
english_devnagari: tasya cākranditaṃ śrutvā gopālāḥ sahasotthitāḥ grastaṃ ca dṛṣṭvā vibhrāntāḥ sarpaṃ vivyadhurulmukaiḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ gras class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ gras class 1 verb]

translation: Hearing his shriek, cowherds suddenly awoke. Beholding Nanda swallowed by the serpent, they got bewildered and struck the serpent with fire-brands.

devanagari: अलातैर्दह्यमानोऽपि नामुञ्चत्तमुरङ्गमः । तमस्पृशत्पदाभ्येत्य भगवान्सात्वतां पतिः ॥ ८ ॥
english_devnagari: alātairdahyamāno tamaspṛśatpadābhyetya bhagavānsātvatāṃ patiḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ spṛś class 6 verb], [vocative single from √ spṛś class 6 verb], [accusative single from √ spṛś class 6 verb]

translation: Though it was scorched by burning fuel, the serpent did not let go Nanda. At that time Kṛṣṇa, the Lord of Sātvatas, approached the reptile and touched it with his foot.

devanagari: स वै भगवतः श्रीमत्पादस्पर्शहताशुभः । भेजे सर्पवपुर्हित्वा रूपं विद्याधरार्चितम् ॥ ९ ॥
english_devnagari: sa vai bhagavataḥ śrīmatpādasparśahatāśubhaḥ bheje sarpavapurhitvā rūpaṃ vidyādharārcitam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [present middle first single], [imperative middle first single]

translation: With all his sins absolved by the touch of the glorious Lord of Lakṣmī, he cast off his serpentine body and assumed a form that was worshipped by Vidyādharas.

devanagari: तमपृच्छद्धृषीकेशः प्रणतं समवस्थितम् । दीप्यमानेन वपुषा पुरुषं हेममालिनम् ॥ १० ॥
english_devnagari: tamapṛcchaddhṛṣīkeśaḥ praṇataṃ samavasthitam dīpyamānena vapuṣā puruṣaṃ hemamālinam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ praś class 6 verb], [vocative single from √ praś class 6 verb], [accusative single from √ praś class 6 verb]

translation: The Lord (of senses), Kṛṣṇa, asked the man with resplendent personality decorated with a wreath of gold, as he reverentially bowed down to him.

devanagari: को भवान्परया लक्ष्म्या रोचतेऽद्भुतदर्शनः । कथं जुगुप्सितामेतां गतिं वा प्रापितोऽवशः ॥ ११ ॥
english_devnagari: ko bhavānparayā lakṣmyā rocate kathaṃ jugupsitāmetāṃ gatiṃ vā prāpito
translation: “Who are you of miraculous form resplendent with supreme splendour? How is it that you lost your self-control and were forced into this disgustful form of life?”

devanagari: सर्प उवाच । अहं विद्याधरः कश्चित्सुदर्शन इति श्रुतः । श्रिया स्वरूपसम्पत्त्या विमानेनाचरन्दिशः ॥ १२ ॥
english_devnagari: sarpa uvāca ahaṃ vidyādharaḥ kaścitsudarśana iti śrutaḥ śriyā svarūpasampattyā vimānenācarandiśaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation:
The serpent (now a Vidyādhara) answered:
“I am a certain (famous) Vidyādhara known as Sudarśana. Endowed with divine splendour and excellent beauty, I ranged in my aerial car in all directions.

devanagari: ऋषीन्विरूपाङ्गिरसः प्राहसं रूपदर्पितः । तैरिमां प्रापितो योनिं प्रलब्धैः स्वेन पाप्मना ॥ १३ ॥
english_devnagari: ṛṣīnvirūpāṅgirasaḥ prāhasaṃ rūpadarpitaḥ tairimāṃ prāpito yoniṃ pralabdhaiḥ svena pāpmanā
translation: Puffed with pride of my beautiful form, I jeered at the ugly forms of the sages Aṅgiras. For my sin of deriding them, I was brought down to this species by them.

devanagari: शापो मेऽनुग्रहायैव कृतस्तैः करुणात्मभिः । यदहं लोकगुरुणा पदा स्पृष्टो हताशुभः ॥ १४ ॥
english_devnagari: śāpo me yadahaṃ lokaguruṇā padā spṛṣṭo hatāśubhaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ kṛ class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ kṛ class 2 verb], [nominative single from √ kṛ class 5 verb], [nominative single from √ kṛ class 8 verb]
[nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ i class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ i class 2 verb]
[vocative single from √ han class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ han class 2 verb]
[vocative single from √ han class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ han class 2 verb]
[nominative single from √ han class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ han class 2 verb]

translation: I now find that this curse was pronounced upon me to show me grace, by those merciful sages, inasmuch as I am absolved of all sins by being touched with foot by the preceptor of the world.

devanagari: तं त्वाहं भवभीतानां प्रपन्नानां भयापहम् । आपृच्छे शापनिर्मुक्तः पादस्पर्शादमीवहन् ॥ १५ ॥
english_devnagari: taṃ tvāhaṃ bhavabhītānāṃ prapannānāṃ bhayāpaham āpṛcche śāpanirmuktaḥ pādasparśādamīvahan
translation: Oh destroyer of sins! I am relieved of the curse by the touch of Your foot. You are the dispeller of the fears of beings who resort to you through fear of saṃsāra. Kindly give me permission to take leave of You.

devanagari: प्रपन्नोऽस्मि महायोगिन्महापुरुष सत्पते । अनुजानीहि मां देव सर्वलोकेश्वरेश्वर ॥ १६ ॥
english_devnagari: prapanno anujānīhi māṃ deva sarvalokeśvareśvara
translation: Oh great yogin! Oh supreme Person! I approach you most humbly, Oh Protector of the righteous people! Oh. resplendent God! Oh Supreme Ruler of the rulers of worlds! Be pleased to grant me permission (to return to my region). Oh Acyuta! Instantaneously at your very sight, I am released from the curse of the Brāhmaṇa.

devanagari: ब्रह्मदण्डाद्विमुक्तोऽहं सद्यस्तेऽच्युत दर्शनात् । यन्नाम गृह्णन्नखिलान्श्रोतॄनात्मानमेव च । सद्यः पुनाति किं भूयस्तस्य स्पृष्टः पदा ह...
translation: A person who utters your name at once purifies all the listeners (of the Name) as well as himself. What more need be said that a person who is touched by your foot gets absolved of all his sins instantaneously.”

devanagari: इत्यनुज्ञाप्य दाशार्हं परिक्रम्याभिवन्द्य च । सुदर्शनो दिवं यातः कृच्छ्रान्नन्दश्च मोचितः ॥ १८ ॥
english_devnagari: ityanujñāpya dāśārhaṃ parikramyābhivandya ca sudarśano divaṃ yātaḥ kṛcchrānnandaśca mocitaḥ
translation: Taking leave of Kṛṣṇa (the descendant of Daśārha) with these words, circumabulating him and playing obeisance to him, Sudarśana went to heaven and Nanda was thus rescued from that precarious situation.

devanagari: निशाम्य कृष्णस्य तदात्मवैभवं । व्रजौकसो विस्मितचेतसस्ततः । समाप्य तस्मिन्नियमं पुनर्व्रजं । नृपाययुस्तत्कथयन्त आदृताः ॥ १९ ॥
english_devnagari: niśāmya kṛṣṇasya tadātmavaibhavaṃ vrajaukaso vismitacetasastataḥ samāpya tasminniyamaṃ punarvrajaṃ nṛpāyayustatkathayanta ādṛtāḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative plural from √ kath class 10 verb], [vocative plural from √ kath class 10 verb]

translation: Having seen the special personal glory of Kṛṣṇa, the inhabitants of Vraja with astonished minds, completed their religious observance of Śivarātri. They returned to Vraja reverentially discussing his glorious exploits.

devanagari: कदाचिदथ गोविन्दो रामश्चाद्भुतविक्रमः । विजह्रतुर्वने रात्र्यां मध्यगौ व्रजयोषिताम् ॥ २० ॥
english_devnagari: kadācidatha govindo rāmaścādbhutavikramaḥ vijahraturvane rātryāṃ madhyagau vrajayoṣitām
translation: On another occasion, Govinda (Kṛṣṇa) and Balarāma of wonderful prowess, sauntered in the forest at night in the company of damsels of Vraja.

devanagari: उपगीयमानौ ललितं स्त्रीजनैर्बद्धसौहृदैः । स्वलङ्कृतानुलिप्ताङ्गौ स्रग्विनौ विरजोऽम्बरौ ॥ २१ ॥
english_devnagari: upagīyamānau lalitaṃ strījanairbaddhasauhṛdaiḥ svalaṅkṛtānuliptāṅgau sragvinau virajo

translation: Being melodiously extolled by damsels, who were deeply attached to them, with their persons well adorned and beautifully painted with sandal-paste and pigments, they wore beautiful wreaths and were dressed in fine garments.

devanagari: निशामुखं मानयन्तावुदितोडुपतारकम् । मल्लिकागन्धमत्तालि जुष्टं कुमुदवायुना ॥ २२ ॥
english_devnagari: niśāmukhaṃ mānayantāvuditoḍupatārakam mallikāgandhamattāli juṣṭaṃ kumudavāyunā

translation: They hailed the advent of the night, as the moon and the stars were rising and appeared in view and as a gentle breeze was blowing over the bed of lilies to which were attracted swarms of bees intoxicated with the fragrance of jasmine flowers.

devanagari: जगतुः सर्वभूतानां मनःश्रवणमङ्गलम् । तौ कल्पयन्तौ युगपत्स्वरमण्डलमूर्च्छितम् ॥ २३ ॥
english_devnagari: jagatuḥ sarvabhūtānāṃ manaḥśravaṇamaṅgalam tau kalpayantau yugapatsvaramaṇḍalamūrcchitam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative dual from √ kḷp ], [vocative dual from √ kḷp ], [accusative dual from √ kḷp ]

translation: Going simultaneously through the whole scale of musical notes with modulations of their voices, both of them presented vocal music which was auspicious and delightful to the mind and ears of all beings in the world.

devanagari: गोप्यस्तद्गीतमाकर्ण्य मूर्च्छिता नाविदन्नृप । स्रंसद्दुकूलमात्मानं स्रस्तकेशस्रजं ततः ॥ २४ ॥
english_devnagari: gopyastadgītamākarṇya mūrcchitā nāvidannṛpa sraṃsaddukūlamātmānaṃ srastakeśasrajaṃ tataḥ
translation: Hearing that ravishing music, the cowherd women fell in a trance and were not aware of their garments slipping away from their persons, loosening of their braids and dropping of wreaths from them, Oh Protector of the people!

devanagari: एवं विक्रीडतोः स्वैरं गायतोः सम्प्रमत्तवत् । शङ्खचूड इति ख्यातो धनदानुचरोऽभ्यगात् ॥ २५ ॥
english_devnagari: evaṃ vikrīḍatoḥ svairaṃ gāyatoḥ sampramattavat śaṅkhacūḍa iti khyāto dhanadānucaro

translation: While they both were thus sporting as they pleased and singing at will like intoxicated persons, a servant of Kubera known as Śaṅkhacūḍa approached them.

devanagari: तयोर्निरीक्षतो राजंस्तन्नाथं प्रमदाजनम् । क्रोशन्तं कालयामास दिश्युदीच्यामशङ्कितः ॥ २६ ॥
english_devnagari: tayornirīkṣato rājaṃstannāthaṃ pramadājanam krośantaṃ kālayāmāsa diśyudīcyāmaśaṅkitaḥ

translation: Before their very eyes (and in defiance of them), he dauntlessly drove the screaming young women who were under their protection, towards the north, Oh king!

devanagari: क्रोशन्तं कृष्ण रामेति विलोक्य स्वपरिग्रहम् । यथा गा दस्युना ग्रस्ता भ्रातरावन्वधावताम् ॥ २७ ॥
english_devnagari: krośantaṃ kṛṣṇa rāmeti vilokya svaparigraham yathā gā dasyunā grastā bhrātarāvanvadhāvatām

translation: Beholding their women crying out (for their succour), “Oh Kṛṣṇa! Oh Balarāma” like cows when seized by a robber, the two brothers rushed in pursuit.

devanagari: मा भैष्टेत्यभयारावौ शालहस्तौ तरस्विनौ । आसेदतुस्तं तरसा त्वरितं गुह्यकाधमम् ॥ २८ ॥
english_devnagari: mā bhaiṣṭetyabhayārāvau śālahastau tarasvinau āsedatustaṃ tarasā tvaritaṃ guhyakādhamam

translation: Shouting out the promise of protection, “Don’t be afraid” the two brothers armed themselves with a Śāla tree and rushing hastily, they overtook the wretch of a Guhyaka (Yaksa)

devanagari: स वीक्ष्य तावनुप्राप्तौ कालमृत्यू इवोद्विजन् । विसृज्य स्त्रीजनं मूढः प्राद्रवज्जीवितेच्छया ॥ २९ ॥
english_devnagari: sa vīkṣya tāvanuprāptau kālamṛtyū ivodvijan visṛjya strījanaṃ mūḍhaḥ prādravajjīvitecchayā

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ prā class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ prā class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ prā class 2 verb]

translation: Terrified to see them both close on his heels like the time-spirit and god of death incarnate, the foolish fellow let the women go and took to his heels with the intention of saving his own life.

devanagari: तमन्वधावद्गोविन्दो यत्र यत्र स धावति । जिहीर्षुस्तच्छिरोरत्नं तस्थौ रक्षन्स्त्रियो बलः ॥ ३० ॥
english_devnagari: tamanvadhāvadgovindo yatra yatra sa dhāvati jihīrṣustacchiroratnaṃ tasthau rakṣanstriyo balaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[nominative single from √ rakṣ class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ rakṣ class 1 verb]

translation: In order to deprive the Yakṣa of the jewel on his head, Kṛṣṇa pursued him wherever he ran, while Balarāma stayed on protecting the women.

devanagari: अविदूर इवाभ्येत्य शिरस्तस्य दुरात्मनः । जहार मुष्टिनैवाङ्ग सहचूडमणिं विभुः ॥ ३१ ॥
english_devnagari: avidūra ivābhyetya śirastasya durātmanaḥ jahāra muṣṭinaivāṅga sahacūḍamaṇiṃ vibhuḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation: Within a short distance, the Lord confronted him and with a blow of his fist sundered off his head along with the jewel.

devanagari: शङ्खचूडं निहत्यैवं मणिमादाय भास्वरम् । अग्रजायाददात्प्रीत्या पश्यन्तीनां च योषिताम् ॥ ३२ ॥
english_devnagari: śaṅkhacūḍaṃ nihatyaivaṃ maṇimādāya bhāsvaram agrajāyādadātprītyā paśyantīnāṃ ca yoṣitām
translation: Killing Śaṅkhacūḍa in this way and taking from him the brilliant gem, Kṛṣṇa gave it, out of affection, to his elder brother, before the very eyes of the damsels.
